info.title: Montimage CTI Checker API
info.description:
REST API for **Montimage's Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) Checker**.

The service exposes the data collected by the Montimage CTI framework — malicious IPs and subnets aggregated from public blocklists (Spamhaus, DShield, CINS, Emerging Threats, Feodo Tracker, blocklist.de), honeypot probes (Dionaea for malware capture, Heralding for credential capture) and partner threat-intel feeds (AbuseIPDB). It also produces time-series visualisations, accepts STIX 2.1 bundles from partners, and provides LLM-powered reputation analysis on top of the aggregated data.

## Common workflows
- **Is this address dangerous?** → `GET /check/{address}` (IP, CIDR subnet or domain)
- **Has this credential been seen on our honeypots?** → `GET /password/{value}` or `GET /username/{value}`
- **How often has this address been observed over time?** → `GET /chart/address/{address}` (PNG) or `GET /chartPath/address/{address}` (URL)
- **Get an LLM-generated reputation report** → `POST /analysis/`
- **Push STIX 2.1 bundles** → `POST /stix` (requires `X-API-Key`)

## Conventions
- All endpoints return JSON unless explicitly documented otherwise (chart PNGs).
- Path parameters containing a `/` (CIDR subnets) **must be URL-encoded** — pass `192.168.0.0/24` as `192.168.0.0%2F24`.
- An empty `[]` response means the lookup succeeded but no records matched — it does **not** indicate an error.

tags.name: Address Checker
tags.description: Look up IP addresses, CIDR subnets, and domain names against the aggregated dangerous-host database (over 1,000 records sourced from public blocklists and Montimage honeypots).

tags.name: Credential Checker
tags.description: Look up usernames and passwords observed on Montimage's credential-capture honeypots (Heralding). Useful for password-hygiene checks and threat hunting.

tags.name: Chart Generator
tags.description: Render time-series charts (PNG line graphs) showing how often a given address or credential has been observed, grouped by day, week or month.

tags.name: Analysis
tags.description: LLM-powered reputation analysis for IP addresses. Aggregates multi-source intelligence, generates a human-readable report via a local Gemma 3 model, and lets users submit and retrieve feedback to improve future analyses.

tags.name: STIX Import
tags.description: Accept STIX 2.1 bundles from authenticated partner systems for ingestion into the threat database. Supports vulnerability, identity, report, threat-actor, malware and other STIX object types.

get.summary: Check an IP, subnet or domain
get.description:
Looks up the supplied address against the dangerous-host database. The input may be:
- an **IPv4 address** (e.g. `8.8.8.8`) — exact match plus any flagged subnet that contains it,
- a **CIDR subnet** (e.g. `192.168.0.0/24`) — pass the `/` URL-encoded as `%2F`,
- a **domain name** (e.g. `example.com`) — resolved to an IPv4 via DNS, then checked as an IP.

Returns an array of source records (empty if not flagged).

parameters.name: ADDRESS
parameters.description: IPv4 address, CIDR subnet (URL-encoded), or domain name.

200.description: Array of source records matching the address (empty array if not flagged).

400.description: The supplied value is not a valid IPv4 address, CIDR subnet, or domain.

500.description: Internal error (e.g. database unreachable, DNS lookup failed).

get.summary: Check an IPv4 address (exact match, ignores subnets)
get.description: Looks up an exact IPv4 address. Unlike `/check`, this endpoint does **not** consider subnet entries — use it when you specifically want to know whether the IP itself has been reported, not whether it falls within a flagged CIDR range.

200.description: Array of source records that explicitly list the IP.

get.description:
Looks up an exact CIDR subnet entry (e.g. `192.168.0.0/24`).

**Important:** the `/` in the CIDR notation must be URL-encoded as `%2F`. For example: `/subnet/192.168.0.0%2F24`.

parameters.name: SUBNET
parameters.description: CIDR subnet, with the slash URL-encoded as `%2F`.

200.description: Array of source records matching the subnet.

get.summary: Check whether a password appears in honeypot captures
get.description: Returns every credential-capture record in which the supplied password was attempted by an attacker against a Montimage Heralding honeypot. Useful for password-hygiene checks ("has my candidate password been seen in the wild?") and for threat hunting.

200.description: Array of credential records that include this password.

get.summary: Check whether a username appears in honeypot captures
get.description: Returns every credential-capture record in which the supplied username was attempted by an attacker against a Montimage Heralding honeypot.

200.description: Array of credential records that include this username.

get.summary: Time-series PNG chart of address occurrences
get.description:
Generates a PNG line chart showing how many sources have listed the given IP, subnet or domain over time, grouped by day, week or month.

Underneath, the endpoint calls `/check/{ADDRESS}` to gather all source records and then aggregates them into a time series rendered with Chart.js.

parameters.name: ADDRESS
parameters.description: IPv4 address, CIDR subnet (URL-encoded) or domain name.

parameters.name: GROUPBY
parameters.description: Aggregation interval. Defaults to `day` when omitted.

400.description: Invalid address parameter or internal rendering error.

get.summary: Time-series chart of address occurrences (returns URL)
get.description: Identical to `/chart/address/{ADDRESS}` but returns the URL path of the rendered PNG (under `/imgs/...`) instead of streaming the image itself. Useful when embedding the chart in a web UI as an `` source without fetching the bytes twice.

parameters.name: ADDRESS
parameters.description: IPv4 address, CIDR subnet (URL-encoded) or domain name.

parameters.name: GROUPBY
parameters.description: Aggregation interval. Defaults to `day` when omitted.

200.description: JSON object containing the URL path of the rendered chart.

400.description: Invalid address parameter or internal rendering error.

get.summary: Time-series PNG chart of credential occurrences
get.description: Generates a PNG line chart showing how often the given credential (matched against **both** captured usernames and passwords) has been observed over time on Montimage Heralding honeypots, grouped by day, week or month.

parameters.name: CREDENTIAL
parameters.description: Credential value (matched against usernames and passwords).

parameters.name: GROUPBY
parameters.description: Aggregation interval. Defaults to `day` when omitted.

post.summary: Generate an LLM-powered reputation report for an IP
post.description:
Aggregates multi-source intelligence about the supplied IP (AbuseIPDB, VirusTotal, Shodan, AlienVault OTX) and feeds it — together with any previously-submitted user feedback for the same IP — to a local Gemma 3 model running on the LLM service.

Returns both the raw multi-source data (`fetchData`) and the generated natural-language analysis (`Result`). Latency typically 2–15 s depending on the model's load.

userPrompt.description: Optional natural-language instruction to bias the analysis (e.g. "focus on reputation, one short paragraph"). Defaults to a generic analyse-this-IP prompt.
userPrompt.example: Please analyse this IP and provide a brief reputation report.

fetchData.description: Raw multi-source data gathered for the IP.

500.description: Upstream data fetch failed, or the LLM service was unreachable.

post.summary: Submit feedback on an analysis result
post.description: Records a rating (1–5) and optional free-text feedback for an LLM-generated analysis. This feedback is fed back into future `/analysis/` calls for the same IP so the model can learn from user corrections.

ipAddress.description: IP that the analysis was about.

summary.description: The analysis result being rated (verbatim, so the feedback stays attached to the exact text).
summary.example: This IP is clean and belongs to Google's public DNS.

feedback.example: Clear and accurate, but could mention the AS.

get.description: Returns stored feedback entries, optionally filtered by IP. The `type` parameter selects between most recent (`latest`), highest-rated (`best`) or lowest-rated (`worst`).

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: If supplied, only feedback for this IP is returned.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return.

post.summary: Import a STIX 2.1 bundle from a partner
post.description:
Accepts a STIX 2.1 bundle and stores it (with per-type object counts) in the threat database. Supports any STIX 2.1 object type — `vulnerability`, `identity`, `report`, `threat-actor`, `malware`, `indicator`, etc.

This endpoint is **authenticated**: include a valid partner key in the `X-API-Key` header. The allowed keys are configured via the `ALLOWED_API_KEYS` environment variable.

400.description: Body is missing or is not a STIX bundle (`type` must be `bundle`).

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Partner API key for authentication. Required by the STIX import endpoint.

AddressItem.description: One source record indicating that an address (IP or CIDR subnet) appeared in a threat-intel feed at a given time.

source.description: URL of the threat-intel feed, or the name of the internal honeypot, that contributed the record.

type.description: Whether this record lists an individual IP or a CIDR subnet.

updated.description: Date or datetime when the source feed was last published. Format varies by source: either `YYYY-MM-DD` or `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`.

data.description: The specific matching IP or subnet (filled in from the query — not necessarily the full list stored in the source feed).

CredentialItem.description: One credential-capture record from a Heralding honeypot session.

source.description: Name of the honeypot that produced the record.

destination_port.description: TCP/UDP port the attacker connected to.

protocol.description: Application-layer protocol exposed by the honeypot.
